questions.question: Write the Q-learning update rule and explain each component of the equation.
keyPoints: Q(S,A) <- Q(S,A) + alpha * [R + gamma * max_a Q(S',a) - Q(S,A)]

questions.question: Why is Q-learning called an off-policy algorithm? What are the advantages of off-policy learning?
keyPoints: Learns greedy policy while following different behavior policy
max_a makes it independent of actions actually taken
Advantages: can learn from any data, experience replay possible

questions.question: Write the complete Q-learning algorithm with pseudocode.
Under what conditions does Q-learning converge to the optimal Q*? Explain each condition.
keyPoints: All state-action pairs visited infinitely often
Learning rate conditions: sum(alpha) = inf, sum(alpha^2) < inf

questions.question: Compare Q-learning with value iteration. What are the key differences?
keyPoints: VI requires model, Q-learning is model-free
VI uses exact transitions, Q-learning uses samples
VI sweeps all states, Q-learning updates visited states
